qa_pairs.question: What is the main goal of this document?
qa_pairs.answer: This document aims to describe how to migrate virtual machine (VM) instances from the legacy Arbutus Cloud to the new Arbutus Cloud.

qa_pairs.question: How should users determine their migration schedule for instances?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should migrate their instances according to their own application requirements and schedule, as they know their workload best.

qa_pairs.question: Why is migration necessary for cloud resources on the legacy Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: Migration is necessary for all cloud resources on the legacy Arbutus Cloud because the legacy Arbutus Cloud will be decommissioned in 2026.

qa_pairs.question: What types of cloud resources require migration from the legacy Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: All cloud resources, including instances, storage volumes, object storage containers, networks, and keys, require migration from the legacy Arbutus Cloud.

qa_pairs.question: What is the deadline for RAS and RAC Projects to complete migration?
qa_pairs.answer: The deadline for both RAS and RAC Projects to migrate between Legacy Arbutus and New Arbutus is August 31, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: Who is responsible for choosing the appropriate migration method?
qa_pairs.answer: You and your research team need to select the migration approach(es) appropriate for your research project.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I have questions about my migration plan or want to review it with an Arbutus Cloud team member?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have questions or wish to review your migration plan with an Arbutus Cloud team member, you should contact cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first step in planning your cloud migration?
qa_pairs.answer: To plan your migration, you need to identify which resources in your legacy Arbutus cloud project need migration, as not all resources may require it.

qa_pairs.question: What is the difference between volume-backed and ephemeral instances?
qa_pairs.answer: Volume-backed instances boot from a volume (e.g., /dev/vda) and may have additional volumes attached, while ephemeral instances do not boot from a volume.

qa_pairs.question: How should volumes larger than 150 GB be migrated?
qa_pairs.answer: Volumes larger than 150 GB should be migrated using Globus.

qa_pairs.question: What should be done if automated deployment systems like Terraform or Ansible were used on the legacy Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have used automation tools like Terraform or Ansible on the legacy Arbutus Cloud, those automation tools should also be used in the process of migration.

qa_pairs.question: Why do custom DNS entries need to be updated during migration?
qa_pairs.answer: Custom DNS entries will need to be updated because your IP addresses will change, as the new Arbutus Cloud uses different floating IP address ranges than the legacy Arbutus Cloud.

qa_pairs.question: What tools can be used to manage Arbutus Cloud resources for migration?
qa_pairs.answer: You can use the OpenStack Dashboard (Horizon Web UI) for simple migrations, or the OpenStack Command Line Interface (CLI) for more complex migrations.

qa_pairs.question: Is OpenStack account sharing allowed?
qa_pairs.answer: No, OpenStack account sharing is strictly forbidden.

qa_pairs.question: Where can someone apply for an OpenStack account?
qa_pairs.answer: Any person who requires an OpenStack account should apply at https://www.alliancecan.ca/en/our-services/advanced-research-computing/account-management/apply-account.

qa_pairs.question: What is the potential duration of an outage needed for migration?
qa_pairs.answer: Depending on the scope of what needs to be migrated, an outage could range from a couple of hours to a couple of days.

qa_pairs.question: What is the process for migrating a RAS project?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have a RAS project, you will need to submit a request to migrate your project to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What should be done with a project on Legacy Arbutus Cloud after migration is complete?
qa_pairs.answer: Once you have completed your migration, you should submit a ticket to request your project on Legacy Arbutus be decommissioned.

qa_pairs.question: What is the URL for the Legacy Arbutus Cloud Horizon Web UI?
qa_pairs.answer: The URL for the Legacy Arbutus Cloud Horizon Web UI is https://arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is the URL for the New Arbutus Cloud Horizon Web UI?
qa_pairs.answer: The URL for the New Arbutus Cloud Horizon Web UI is https://arbutus.alliancecan.ca/.

qa_pairs.question: Which web browsers are supported for accessing the Arbutus Cloud Horizon Web UI?
qa_pairs.answer: Firefox and Chrome browsers are supported. Safari and Edge may work but have not been validated.

qa_pairs.question: What Arbutus Cloud resources will be pre-created for users?
qa_pairs.answer: Your Arbutus Cloud Project (Tenant), Network, and Router will be pre-created for you in Arbutus Cloud.

qa_pairs.question: Will I have access to the same projects on the New Arbutus Cloud as I did on the Legacy Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you will have access to the same projects on the New Arbutus Cloud as you had on the Legacy Arbutus Cloud.

qa_pairs.question: Why might new Security Groups be required in the New Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: New Security Groups (OpenStack’s firewall rules) may be required because the floating IP range in the New Arbutus Cloud is different than the Legacy Arbutus cloud.
